title: podman build fail to pull images.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** Trying to build the from https://github.com/openshift/installer/blob/master/images/nested-libvirt/Dockerfile dockerfile. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Get the above mentioned Dockerfile 2. It uses the private registry but I already put the auth to the ~/.docker/config file. 3. Start the build **Describe the results you received:** ```console $ podman build -t test:test -f Dockerfile . STEP 1: FROM build Error: error creating build container: The following failures happened while trying to pull image specified by "build" based on search registries in /etc/containers/registries.conf: * "localhost/build": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://localhost/build:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://localhost/v2/: dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused * "docker.io/library/build": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://build:latest: Error reading manifest latest in docker.io/library/build: errors: denied: requested access to the resource is denied unauthorized: authentication required * "registry.fedoraproject.org/build": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/build:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.fedoraproject.org/build: manifest unknown: manifest unknown * "quay.io/build": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://quay.io/build:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/build: error parsing HTTP 404 response body: invalid character '<' looking for beginning of value: "\n404 Not Found\n

Not Found

\n

The requested URL was not found on the server. If you entered the URL manually please check your spelling and try again.

\n" * "registry.access.redhat.com/build": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/build:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/build: name unknown: Repo not found * "registry.centos.org/build": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.centos.org/build:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.centos.org/build: manifest unknown: manifest unknown ``` **Describe the results you expected:** Should be successfully build it. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** **Output of `podman version`:** ``` $ podman version Version: 1.2.0 RemoteAPI Version: 1 Go Version: go1.11.5 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` $ podman info --debug debug: compiler: gc git commit: "" go version: go1.11.5 podman version: 1.2.0 host: BuildahVersion: 1.7.2 Conmon: package: podman-1.2.0-2.git3bd528e.fc29.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: d88bb0e63cb70f9787a8e410716924f380af361f' Distribution: distribution: fedora version: "29" MemFree: 1171578880 MemTotal: 33567670272 OCIRuntime: package: runc-1.0.0-68.dev.git6635b4f.fc29.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/runc version: |- runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev commit: ef9132178ccc3d2775d4fb51f1e431f30cac1398-dirty spec: 1.0.1-dev SwapFree: 16408907776 SwapTotal: 16850612224 arch: amd64 cpus: 8 hostname: localhost.localdomain kernel: 5.0.6-200.fc29.x86_64 os: linux rootless: true uptime: 509h 18m 15.57s (Approximately 21.21 days) insecure registries: registries: [] registries: registries: - docker.io - registry.fedoraproject.org - quay.io - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.centos.org store: ConfigFile: /home/prkumar/.config/containers/storage.conf ContainerStore: number: 8 GraphDriverName: overlay GraphOptions: - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs GraphRoot: /home/prkumar/.local/share/containers/storage GraphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" ImageStore: number: 17 RunRoot: /run/user/1000 VolumePath: /home/prkumar/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes ``` **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Is this Dockerfile correct?  
It seems to do a 

from ... as **builder**

But later it does

COPY --from=**build** 

I think the second is supposed to be builder?  The run looks like it is attempting to pull docker.io/library/build, which seems to be protected image.

Thanks @rhatdan , I was not able to figure out why it is failing from the error. I should've looks closely to it. Will send the PR to that dockerfile to fix it. 

Closing it.
